properties.areaDesc: Cabo Rojo, PR; Hormigueros, PR; Mayaguez, PR
properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued April 19 at 9:23PM AST until April 20 at 7:00AM AST by NWS San Juan PR
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...Flooding caused by standing water in roads along Rio
Guanajibo.

* WHEN...Until 700 AM AST Monday.

* IMPACTS...Water over roadways. Overflowing poor drainage areas.
Some low-water crossings may become impassable. River or stream
flows are elevated.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 919 PM AST, Emergency management indicated that roads
PR-103 intersecting PR-114 along Rio Guanajibo in Cabo Rojo
remain closed due to slow moving or standing water. This
advisory will remain in place while the threat continues, and
may be upgraded to a warning, if conditions warrant.
- Flooding impacts will continue, but no additional rainfall is
expected.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
properties.instruction:
Turn around, don't drown when encountering flooded roads. Most flood
deaths occur in vehicles.

Be especially cautious at night when it is harder to recognize the
dangers of flooding.

Please report observed flooding to local emergency services or law
enforcement and request they pass this information to the National
Weather Service when you can do so safely.

NWSheadline: FLOOD ADVISORY IN EFFECT UNTIL 7 AM AST MONDAY

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued April 19 at 6:58PM AST until April 20 at 7:00AM AST by NWS San Juan PR
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by standing water in roads along Rio
Culebrinas.

* WHERE...Aguada.

* WHEN...Until 700 AM AST Monday.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
Water over roadways. Overflowing poor drainage areas. Some
low-water crossings may become impassable.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 652 PM AST, emergency managers indicated that roads
PR-418, and PR-115 along Rio Culebrinas in Aguada remain with
slow moving or standing water. PR-418 is completely
impassable and remains closed. PR-115 is only partially
transitable with km 26.1 to 26.3 remaining closed. PR-439 on
the other hand is now reported as transitable.
- This advisory will remain in place while the threat
continues, and may be upgraded to a warning, if conditions
warrant.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
properties.instruction:
Turn around, don't drown when encountering flooded roads. Most flood
deaths occur in vehicles.

Be especially cautious at night when it is harder to recognize the
dangers of flooding.

Be aware of your surroundings and do not drive on flooded roads.

NWSheadline: FLOOD ADVISORY NOW IN EFFECT UNTIL 7 AM AST MONDAY

title: Current watches, warnings, and advisories for Puerto Rico
